title: [InstCombine] Do not use operand info in `replaceInInstruction`
summary:
The generalization centers on enabling speculative execution of instructions even when their variable operands are being replaced, which previously blocked optimizations. The key change is adding a `UseVariableInfo` parameter to `isSafeToSpeculativelyExecute`, allowing the safety check to ignore variable-specific constraints (like load alignment) when those operands will be substituted with constants. A new helper, `isSafeToSpeculativelyExecuteWithVariableReplaced`, sets this flag to false, permitting transformations like folding `select` when the condition variable is replaced by a known constant.

This expands the pattern from a single case (non-speculatable calls) to handle speculatable calls, intrinsics, loads with known alignment, and bitwise operations, as shown in the test cases. The fix ensures the optimization only applies when the replacement value is a constant and the instruction has one use, maintaining safety. For developers, this demonstrates parameterizing safety checks to separate operand-specific constraints from intrinsic instruction hazards, enabling more aggressive folding when operands become constants.
